public_reputation: Wagon-guard and field defender who is better known by granary families than by any lord.
appearance: Bright-plumed road gear, polished fittings, and visible attention to presentation mark the role before the voice does.
manner: Moves like someone trained to decide quickly once the threshold for action has been crossed. In Zootia, that reads as someone practical, supply-aware, and season-conscious, attentive to material reliability over display, and the habits of a warrior still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of warrior in and around Zootia floodplain circuits, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Zootia floodplain circuits while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Hal Wenner will trade useful tactical knowledge if the party solves a security problem too sensitive for ordinary channels.
Hal Wenner needs help hardening a position, drilling unreliable auxiliaries, or reading an approaching threat around Zootia floodplain circuits.

support_scope_notes: Integrated from the prior_release canon-ready named-NPC ledger append. Unsupported fields remain restrained. Appearance extended under V33_1 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Weapon discipline, drilled stillness, and practiced readiness mark Hal Wenner as someone tied to organized force rather than casual violence.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Zootia floodplain circuits: drill yards, fortifications, command posts, or exposed routes

first_impression: Hal Wenner presents as Moves like someone trained to decide quickly once the threshold for action has been crossed. In Zootia, that reads as someone practical, supply-aware, and season-conscious, attentive to material reliability over display, and the habits of a warrior still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Warrior, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Principality of Zootia.
what_they_refuse: Hal Wenner refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Warrior too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Hal Wenner can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Hal Wenner adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Hal Wenner is Wagon-guard and field defender who is better known by granary families than by any lord. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Warrior.
social_texture: Hal Wenner speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Zootia floodplain circuits, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Hal touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Hal Wenner when the party needs a socially grounded face for Zootia floodplain circuits: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
